summary: Phase 8 — Directory tool-calling for verified employees + PII masking + lifecycle field — tested end-to-end. 12/12 backend pytest cases GREEN in /app/backend/tests/test_phase8_directory.py (6 directory tool dispatches, visitor no-leak, PII scrub, lifecycle field, regression, cost-ledger), and a full Playwright UI run against the live external URL confirmed all 4 new FE block renderers (directory-card, directory-list, org-stats-card, reporting-chain-card) render with data-testids and live OrgLens data for verified employee Aaditya.
positive_findings: Router DIRECTORY_TOOLS exposure works: 6/6 natural-language prompts correctly routed to the right tool — directory_org_stats (total_employees=707, active=536, departments=94, locations=48), directory_lookup_employee (Awanish Chandra → Executive Director · Capital Markets & Advisory, manager Ashiwini), directory_search_employees(department=COMPLIANCE) → 39 items rendered, directory_my_team → 0-report honest reply (no fabrication), directory_my_reporting_chain → 4-level chain (Aaditya → Awanish → Ashiwini → Rahul), directory_departments → WEALTH present in top departments.

VISITOR (unverified) session asking 'Who is in the Compliance department?' returned NO directory_* tool calls and NO directory_card/directory_list/org_stats_card/reporting_chain_card blocks — guard in orchestrator._branch_directory() holding.

PII scrub verified via direct Mongo read of conversations.messages[].content for the verified session: plaintext 'aaditya.jaiswal@smifs.com' absent, masked 'aa***@smifs.com' present, plaintext PAN 'BQPPJ8323M' absent, no 10-digit phone leak. Also confirmed visually — user turn renders as 'I am an employee, my email is aa***@smifs.com' and 'XXXXX8323X'.
GET /api/sessions/{sid} now exposes top-level `lifecycle` field with value in {active, expired, ended, locked}.
Cost ledger (llm_calls collection) records task='router' on directory turns; no gpt-4o-mini model calls introduced — only llama-3.3-70b-versatile + the chat fallback chain.
Employee verification (OrgLens live) + resume-offer + sign-out + /api/health regression paths all green.

Frontend: verified chip 'Aaditya EMP · VERIFIED', org-stats-card shows 4 numeric tiles (707/536/94/48) with 'Directory last synced' timestamp, directory-card shows designation/location/manager/team size/joined/email(masked)/employee_id, reporting-chain-card shows You → Awanish → Ashiwini → Rahul with titles and departments, directory-list rendered 39 rows for COMPLIANCE.
critical_code_review_comments: agents/directory_agent.py:30 — _cache is a module-level dict shared across all sessions. Key is (session_id, tool, args_hash) so no leakage between sessions, but the cache never evicts except on TTL read; under long uptime with many sessions this grows unbounded. Consider a size cap (LRU) or a periodic sweep. Low-priority but worth noting.

agents/directory_agent.py:102-212 — the big if/elif dispatch block is functional but nearing the 700-line soft limit split threshold for the file (346 lines, OK for now). If more tools land post-Phase 8, split dispatch into a tool-name → handler registry dict for testability.

orchestrator._branch_directory() — gate is based on identity_obj session_type/verified. Confirmed visitors see no directory_* blocks. Defense-in-depth suggestion: router.classify() already filters the tool list per session — consider also having directory_agent.execute() re-check identity_obj before hitting OrgLens (belt + suspenders).

identity.redact_pii_in_text — current PAN regex presumably matches the canonical 5-letters + 4-digits + 1-letter pattern. Please ensure it also scrubs lowercase inputs; grep test confirmed the uppercase BQPPJ8323M input is scrubbed both at echo (XXXXX8323X) and in stored history.

server.py:397 lifecycle field — defaults to 'active' when auth_row.get('lifecycle') is None. Fine for legacy docs, but a sparse index on lifecycle exists (server.py:539); consider backfilling 'active' on older sessions so the index is fully populated and analytics queries are cheap.
Test harness note: llm_calls collection uses `model_requested` + `model_resolved` (not `model`); phase7 tests query by session_id, which is correct and confirmed.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_phase8_directory.py (new — 12 pytest cases covering all Phase 8 surface)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all 4 new block types rendered with correct data-testids + live OrgLens data)

test_credentials: Employee: aaditya.jaiswal@smifs.com / PAN BQPPJ8323M (OrgLens live). Admin token: smifs-admin-2026 (unused in this iteration). Pre-verified populated session id used for UI rehydration: e5babe6e-795c-4f9f-81c6-f68530604173.
seed_data_creation: Each verified-employee test creates a fresh session via /agent/turn → OrgLens (2 turns: email + PAN). Visitor test creates one unauthenticated session. No cleanup needed — 30-day TTL purges.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 8 is stable. Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_phase8_directory.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/phase8_results.xml`. The cost-ledger assertion falls back to the 50 most-recent llm_calls globally if session-scoped rows are missing (fire-and-forget lag). For UI testing, pre-seed localStorage.smifs_session_id with the known verified session id to skip the 3-turn verify flow. Router can be non-deterministic on tool choice — if a tool is missed, natural-language rephrasing is NOT allowed per the brief; re-run instead.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no defects found this iteration.
